audit_items.proof_trace: The `list` pointer allocated by `strdup` at line 4629 is indeed not freed on the early-return paths at lines 4647 and 4665. However, `server_sockets()` is called only during startup in `main()`, and both call sites (lines 5746, 5761) call `exit(EX_OSERR)` immediately when the function returns non-zero — the OS reclaims all memory on process exit. Additionally, `settings.inter` is set exclusively from the command-line `-l` option (`optarg`), not from any network or runtime input, so no external attacker can trigger these paths.
